summary.intent: Implement four GitHub issues (#324, #328, #419, #421) adding Heading (with HeadingLevelProvider + Section), Landmark, and Highlight adapter components for both the Leptos and Dioxus frameworks, following the workspace's adapter-component delivery contract.
summary.outcome: All six adapter components were implemented with SSR + wasm tests, E2E fixtures/harnesses, and widget demos across all six widget crates; additional improvements included adding GlobalAttributes pass-through to all existing Dioxus components, proper class-merging for Leptos, TypedChildren adoption, fixture modularization by category, and renaming test files to canonical naming while eliminating an allowlist in the parity lint.

repo: Leptos SSR tests that use provide_context/use_context must be wrapped in Owner::new().with(|| ...) — without an Owner, context is never published and all use_context calls return None.

Leptos attr:class produces a duplicate class= attribute (not merged) when the component also emits an internal class; browsers silently drop one. Fix: pre-merge in the AttrMap layer before converting to attrs using a merge_consumer_class_into helper.

Dioxus components must use explicit #[derive(Props, Clone, PartialEq, Debug)] structs (not function-style props); any type used in a Props struct must implement PartialEq — including agnostic-core Messages types.

Dioxus accepts arbitrary HTML attrs via #[props(extends = GlobalAttributes)] attrs: Vec<Attribute>; spread with ..props.attrs inside rsx!. Leptos equivalent is attr:foo= prefix at call-site, which works automatically without prop declaration (unless the component has its own class, in which case merging is needed).

merge_dioxus_attrs(consumer_attrs, component_attrs) in crates/ars-dioxus/src/as_child.rs tokenizes class/style for additive merge; component attrs win on conflict for non-token attrs (preserving data-ars-* integrity).

The adapter-component-delivery.md spec requires E2E fixture entries, E2E harness modules, and widget demos in all 6 widget crates — these deliverables were omitted from the initial plan and had to be added after user reminder.
E2E fixture main.rs should be split into categories/{mod,navigation,utility,...}.rs so parallel agents implementing different component categories never edit the same file.

Canonical adapter test file naming is test_{component}.rs and test_{component}_wasm.rs. Bare-named files (separator.rs, visually_hidden.rs) were tech debt tracked by a COMPONENT_NAMED_ADAPTER_TEST_FILES allowlist in xtask/src/lint.rs — that allowlist has now been deleted.
The xtask adapter-parity lint's wasm test regex was missing #[wasm_bindgen_test(async)], causing those tests to be silently excluded from parity counts.

use_memo is appropriate for merging consumer attrs only when the component already has a reactive machine/derive (Button, Tabs); stateless components (Heading, Landmark, Highlight, Separator, VisuallyHidden) should merge inline — Memo overhead exceeds merge cost there.
TypedChildren<T> where View<T>: IntoView is the workspace convention for Leptos components taking children; preserves view type for precise reactive diffing and future AddAnyAttr support.

Tailwind preflight and many CSS resets flatten h1-h6 visual sizes to identical; heading widget demos require explicit font-size styling (inline styles or Tailwind text-* classes) to demonstrate the API behavior.

Leptos CSR context in E2E fixtures leaks across sibling components when provider and heading are at the same component level; wrap provider subtrees in their own component to get an isolated owner scope.

HeadingLevelProvider/Section context for an accessible heading hierarchy in E2E fixtures requires careful axe-compliant level ordering (page h1 → fixture h2 → provider h3 → Section h4) to avoid axe heading-order violations.

code.finding: ID counter was a process-global AtomicU64 under cfg(not(wasm32)), causing flaky tests from parallel threads racing on reset_id_counter() and a latent bug where concurrent SSR requests interleaved IDs. Fixed by unifying with the wasm32 path: single thread_local! { Cell<u64> } on all targets.
landmark::Messages struct was missing PartialEq derive; MessageFn supports it but the derive was absent, preventing Messages from being used in Dioxus Props structs.

Added merge_consumer_class_into(&mut AttrMap, Option<&str>) helper that reads existing HtmlAttr::Class, concatenates consumer tokens, and writes a single merged value back — avoids the duplicate class= attribute problem that occurs when using attr:class on a component that already emits class via its own AttrMap.

COMPONENT_NAMED_ADAPTER_TEST_FILES allowlist and its branching logic have been removed. File filter is now name.starts_with('test_') && name.ends_with('.rs'). Test-attribute regex extended to also match #[wasm_bindgen_test(async)].

Canonical pattern for 'use prop if present, else snapshot from signal': both Leptos and Dioxus use_locale() return Signal<Locale>; Leptos reads with .get(), Dioxus with .read().clone(). Locale is snapshotted at Api::new() construction time — mid-life locale changes do not retrigger label resolution (per agnostic-core contract).

workflow: cargo xci-fast (alias for cargo xci --profile fast) is the mandatory workspace pre-push gate covering fmt, leptosfmt, check, clippy, unit, integration, adapter, spec-compile-snippets, error-variant-coverage, mutual-exclusion, and snapshot-count.
leptosfmt must be run separately from cargo +nightly fmt --all; xci-fast enforces both; apply with leptosfmt --edition 2021 **/*.rs and cargo +nightly fmt --all before pushing.
TDD order in this workspace: write failing tests first (confirm failure), implement, re-run (confirm green), then commit per component.
E2E tests require chromedriver running on port 9515 (start in background) and the fixture server compiled + served by trunk (Leptos) or dx serve --web (Dioxus).
GitHub Project issue status must be moved to 'In Progress' at implementation start via gh api (not just creating the PR).
Wasm coverage is a CI-only gate (requires LLVM toolchain); local confirmation is limited to verifying the test binaries execute and spot-checking per-file coverage metrics from lcov.

When adapting existing components to add a new prop, always check whether any test struct literals need the new field added (Dioxus test struct cloning tests fail if Props struct gains non-optional fields).
Use git mv for file renames so git tracks history; naked copy+delete loses blame.

friction: Leptos attr:class silently produces duplicate class= attribute when the component emits its own class — browsers drop one without error, making this a hard-to-diagnose silent failure only discovered via empirical probing.
xci-fast failed multiple times before passing: first on leptosfmt formatting (not applied), then on nightly fmt (applied separately), requiring three attempts total before the gate went green.
Capturing background xci-fast output was unreliable — the output file was not written correctly when run via the task system, requiring a foreground retry with explicit log redirection.

CSR context propagation in the E2E Leptos fixture caused Heading to render at an unexpected level (h3 instead of h1 for a 'default' heading) due to HeadingContext leaking from a sibling provider; required restructuring the fixture into a nested component to isolate scope.
Axe heading-order violations in E2E fixture required careful h1→h2→h3→h4 scaffolding around the demo headings to maintain semantic correctness while still demonstrating the API.

The adapter-component-delivery.md requirements (E2E fixtures, E2E harness modules, widgets examples) were not in the original plan and were only caught after user reminder, requiring a second large implementation pass.
The view! macro turbofish Vec::<String>::new() causes a parse error inside view!; must bind to a local variable first.
Dioxus dx serve output was slow to appear and the process required manual monitoring; determining when the server was actually ready took multiple polling attempts.

CI wasm coverage could not be fully verified locally (requires LLVM 22 / clang-22); the Dioxus wasm-only coverage dropped to 72.8% (under the 77% threshold) before native merge, creating uncertainty about whether the merged threshold would pass.
open_items: button.rs and tabs.rs test files in both ars-leptos and ars-dioxus still use bare naming (not renamed to test_button.rs / test_tabs.rs); they were explicitly left out of scope for this rename pass.
@codex review cycle not yet started — needs to be triggered after first push and iterated until Codex leaves a thumbs-up.
Merged (native + wasm) coverage threshold verification for ars-leptos and ars-dioxus was not completed locally; CI nightly wasm-coverage job will be the actual gate.

Leptos Landmark's locale prop and use_messages/resolve_locale integration was added per spec but the i18n message registry wiring in the fixture is minimal (stub Messages only); production callers will need to wire use_messages() for reactive label resolution.

AsChild variants for Heading, Landmark, and Highlight are not implemented — the adapter specs don't require them, but they may be needed if consumers require full DOM control (e.g., wrapping an existing element). Deferred as a separate task.

The HeadingLevelProvider and Section components are documented in prose in the adapter specs but not enumerated in the Public Adapter API table (§2) of spec/leptos-components/utility/heading.md and the Dioxus equivalent; spec update was noted but not done.

Mid-life locale reactivity for Landmark aria-label is not wired — Api::new() snapshots locale at construction; if locale changes post-mount the label won't update. Noted in plan as intentional deferral per agnostic-core contract.

The plan file at /Users/ericson/.claude/plans/implement-tasks-324-328-tidy-squirrel.md was not updated to reflect the GlobalAttributes, TypedChildren, class-merging, or test-renaming work added after the original scope.
